title: Star Wars Missions 18: Rogue Squadron to the Rescue
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:Missions18.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars Missions 18:
Rogue Squadron to the Rescue'' |author=[[Ryder Windham]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[February]] [[1999]]''[[Star Wars Missions 18: Rogue Squadron to the Rescue]]'' |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=75 |isbn=059084430X |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]''[[Star Wars Missions 17: Darth Vader's Return]]'' and the next three ''Star Wars Missions'' books tell a continuous story that starts with Darth Vader returning to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Though ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' states that Vader did not return to Coruscant until he had discovered Luke Skywalker's identity, [[Empire 14|''Empire'' 14]] depicts Vader returning immediately to Coruscant after crashing on Vaal, a fact which is repeated in ''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]''. |series=''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' |preceded by=''[[Star Wars Missions 17: Darth Vader's Return]]'' |followed by=''[[Star Wars Missions 19: Bounty on Bonadan]]'' }} '''''Star Wars Missions 18: Rogue Squadron to the Rescue''''' is the eighteenth adventure book in the ''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' young-reader series and [[roleplaying game]]. It was published by [[Scholastic]] in [[February]] [[1999]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''The Empire has seized a Corellian Action IV bulk freighter. As a member of Rogue Squadron, you must find out why the Imperial forces have taken the vessel. Then you must stop them. Your mission: to free the freighter's crew from their detention cells, and to infiltrate the hijacked craft.'' ''May the Force be with you.'' ==Plot summary== On [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] meets with [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], and delivers [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin's]] [[Hologram disc/Legends|holotape]]. The holotape proposes the concept of [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] and indicates how they can be created. Palpatine, intrigued, appoints General [[Mordak]] head of the gravity well scheme, and dispatches Vader to [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]], to oversee the creation of the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]. Meanwhile, on the [[Malagarr/Legends|uncharted red planet]], [[Q-7N]] and his [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] allies assess the damage done by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]]. Q-7N finds nothing of value, however. A [[message pod]], sent by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], arrives on the planet, with a message from [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], who requests that [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and her friends join him on [[Kwenn Space Station/Legends|Kwenn Space Station]]. At Kwenn Space Station, Antilles, [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]], and [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] are investigating an [[Action VI bulk transport/Legends|Action VI transport]] that the Empire has detained, the ''[[Stellar Manx]]''. While snooping about, they are set upon by a squad of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. The Rogues split up, and one of them eludes the Imperials by swimming through an aquarium. The lone Rogue encounters Captain [[Kar Lamoran]] in the detention cell, and frees her, along with her crew. Making their way back to Lamoran's Action VI, the Rogue encounters more [[Security droid/Legends|security droids]]. The ship, however, takes off before they can reach it, but the Rogue pursues it, boards it, and takes it back to Kwenn Space Station. During this expedition, the Rogue also finds out that Darth Vader is in charge of the Imperial operation, and that the freighter's destination was [[Bonadan/Legends|Bonadan]]. ==Appearances== {{App
